Why It Exists: Players, recruits, coaches, staff, and created people share a base human identity.
FM/Planning Signal: FM separates people/basic people and player/non-player history.
Implementation Implication: Use Person as the root, then attach athlete, recruit, coach, and staff profiles.

Why It Exists: Scholarships, roster membership, staff contracts, depth chart slots, conference membership, NIL deals, and visits all have lifecycle.
FM/Planning Signal: FM-style object links and transaction/history stores.
Implementation Implication: Use join tables with IDs, status, dates, and history instead of nested copies.

Why It Exists: Profiles need long-term memory without turning live records into giant blobs.
FM/Planning Signal: FM player/non-player history and indexed save stores.
Implementation Implication: Keep current tables lean; append histories and snapshots separately.

Why It Exists: Continue is a system pipeline, not a button that increments a number.
FM/Planning Signal: FM fixture rules, date windows, transfer windows, event deadlines.
Implementation Implication: Model seasons, weeks, phases, windows, blockers, scheduled jobs, and events.

Why It Exists: College rules change and saves must remain stable.
FM/Planning Signal: FM database versions and editor overlays.
Implementation Implication: Store active ruleset version and migration metadata in each save.

Why It Exists: A player should not know true ratings until enough knowledge exists.
FM/Planning Signal: FM scouted person and attribute snapshot signals.
Implementation Implication: Store true ratings separately from scouted snapshots, confidence, and staff bias.

Why It Exists: Every meaningful click should leave a durable trace.
FM/Planning Signal: FM actions/resources plus current action/event log direction.
Implementation Implication: Commands write action log, event log, changed records, and invariants.

Why It Exists: Dense menus need consistent actions, columns, tabs, routes, and inspectors.
